project.name: media-stack
project.description: Self-hosted, Docker Compose-based streaming media platform for sharing home apps (Jellyfin, Immich, the *arr stack, Matrix, and more) with family and friends. Pangolin reverse-proxy plus Keycloak provide unified SSO, social login, and automated TLS so users get a single branded login across services without installing a VPN; each top-level directory is an independent compose project orchestrated by a top-level Makefile.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Starlette middleware and helpers that enforce bearer-token authentication on the MCP HTTP surface while leaving health/discovery paths public.

nodes.name: BearerAuthMiddleware
nodes.summary: Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware that requires a valid bearer token for protected routes, returning 401 JSON for missing/invalid credentials and 503 when no token is configured.

nodes.name: expected_token
nodes.summary: Resolves the expected bearer token by reading the first non-empty value among the configured token environment variables.

nodes.name: is_authorized
nodes.summary: Validates an Authorization header against the expected token using a constant-time comparison to resist timing attacks.

nodes.name: chunking.py
nodes.summary: Text normalization and page-aware chunking utilities that split source pages into overlapping, word-boundary-aligned chunks for indexing.

nodes.name: chunk_page
nodes.summary: Splits a single normalized page of text into overlapping chunks, snapping chunk ends to sentence/word boundaries and advancing the cursor with overlap.

nodes.name: chunk_pages
nodes.summary: Chunks a sequence of pages by delegating to chunk_page and concatenating the resulting chunks across the whole document.

nodes.name: _nearest_boundary
nodes.summary: Finds a clean cut point near a target end offset by preferring the nearest sentence terminator and falling back to whitespace.

nodes.name: embeddings.py
nodes.summary: Embedding provider abstraction with a deterministic hash fallback and a FastEmbed/ONNX implementation that probes OpenVINO and GPU execution providers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Protocol for structural typing and runtime signature inspection (inspect.signature) to feature-detect ONNX provider kwargs.

nodes.name: EmbeddingProvider
nodes.summary: Protocol defining the embedding provider interface: embed_passages, embed_query, and status.

nodes.name: HashEmbeddingProvider
nodes.summary: Deterministic fallback embedding provider that hashes tokens into a fixed-dimension vector via blake2b, used when no ML model is available.

nodes.name: FastEmbedProvider
nodes.summary: Lazy-loading FastEmbed TextEmbedding provider that selects ONNX execution providers (OpenVINO/GPU/CPU) based on available hardware and reports the active backend in status.

nodes.name: build_embedding_provider
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a hash or FastEmbed provider based on the configured backend, with hash as the explicit and auto-mode fallback.

nodes.name: ingest.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that builds or refreshes the Black Book index by invoking BlackBookService.ensure_index and printing the resulting status as JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses a --force flag, runs the service index build, and prints the JSON result; the ingest script's command-line entry point.

nodes.name: mcp_server.py
nodes.summary: FastMCP/Starlette application entry point exposing Black Book retrieval as MCP tools (ask, search, read_citation, status) plus a /healthz route, fronted by bearer-auth middleware and served via uvicorn.

nodes.name: ask_blackbook
nodes.summary: MCP tool that returns a bounded, cited evidence pack for a grow question, delegating to BlackBookService.ask with crop/facility context.

nodes.name: blackbook_search
nodes.summary: MCP tool that runs fts, vector, or hybrid retrieval against the Black Book index via BlackBookService.search.

nodes.name: blackbook_read_citation
nodes.summary: MCP tool that reads one bounded citation chunk by chunk_id via BlackBookService.read_citation.

nodes.name: blackbook_status
nodes.summary: MCP tool that returns source, index, and embedding backend status without leaking secrets.

nodes.name: healthz
nodes.summary: Custom GET /healthz route reporting source existence, index readiness, and auth configuration, returning 200 when healthy and 503 otherwise.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Boots the MCP HTTP app under uvicorn using the configured host, port, and log level.

nodes.name: service.py
nodes.summary: Core orchestration layer that ingests the source PDF, builds the index, and serves hybrid retrieval, citation reads, and evidence-pack answers with confidence notes and reciprocal-rank fusion.

nodes.name: BlackBookService
nodes.summary: Stateful facade coordinating settings, index, and embedding provider to (re)build the index from PDF pages and answer status, search, ask, and read_citation requests with thread-safe rebuilds.

nodes.name: _fuse_hits
nodes.summary: Fuses multiple named hit lists into a ranked result set using reciprocal-rank fusion, tracking which retrieval sources contributed each hit.

nodes.name: _excerpt
nodes.summary: Builds a bounded excerpt window around the first query-term match in a chunk, adding ellipses when the text is truncated.

nodes.name: _confidence_notes
nodes.summary: Produces human-readable confidence caveats based on result count, retrieval mode, and whether the embedding backend fell back to the hash provider.

nodes.name: settings.py
nodes.summary: Defines the immutable Settings dataclass and load_settings, which assembles all runtime configuration (paths, embedding backend, chunking, server) from environment variables.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the full runtime configuration: source/index/cache paths, embedding backend and devices, chunking sizes, and server host/port/log level.

nodes.name: load_settings
nodes.summary: Reads CCI_* environment variables (with defaults) and constructs a Settings instance for the rest of the service.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed index providing FTS5 full-text search, brute-force vector cosine search, chunk storage with embeddings, and metadata, plus serialization helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLite FTS5 for keyword search and numpy float32 blobs for vector storage, computing cosine similarity in Python rather than a vector extension.

nodes.name: BlackBookIndex
nodes.summary: Manages the SQLite index lifecycle: schema creation, rebuild from chunks and embeddings, status/metadata reads, single-chunk reads, FTS5 keyword search, and normalized vector similarity search.

nodes.name: _lib.py
nodes.summary: Shared helper library for the IPTV monitoring probes, providing Apprise notifications, Docker-secret reading, JSON state persistence, healthchecks.io dead-man pings, and a check_main decorator that wraps each probe's entry point with deduplicated failure and recovery alerting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a decorator factory (check_main) returning a functools.wraps wrapper for cross-cutting failure/recovery handling and healthchecks.io pings around each probe's main().

nodes.name: iptv-auth.py
nodes.summary: Canary probe that calls the upstream Xtream Codes player_api and fires a critical alert (with recover-once dedup) when the IPTV subscription auth flag flips to failed.

nodes.name: iptv-canary-epg.py
nodes.summary: Per-channel EPG canary that stream-parses the ~12 MB XMLTV feed for six watched channels and fires a critical alert when any loses future programme coverage, tracking per-channel state transitions so each healthy/failing flip notifies exactly once.
nodes.languageNotes: Streams XMLTV via xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse with elem.clear() to keep memory flat over the multi-megabyte feed while accumulating per-channel metrics.

nodes.name: iptv-channel-count.py
nodes.summary: Probe that fetches the local Xtream Codes live-stream list and alerts when the channel count drops below a configurable floor, recovering once when it returns above the floor.

nodes.name: iptv-epg-freshness.py
nodes.summary: Probe that stream-parses the XMLTV EPG to find the latest programme stop time and warns when guide coverage no longer extends a configurable minimum number of hours into the future.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses iterparse + elem.clear() streaming to bound memory on the ~12 MB XMLTV response since the feed exposes no Last-Modified header.

nodes.name: iptv-renewal-warn.py
nodes.summary: Probe that reads the upstream subscription exp_date and emits an at-most-once-per-day warning as the renewal window approaches expiry, clearing its warn state when outside the window.

nodes.name: read_secret
nodes.summary: Reads and strips a Docker secret file from /run/secrets by name.

nodes.name: notify
nodes.summary: POSTs a tagged title/body notification to the Apprise API, substituting a placeholder body to avoid Apprise's HTTP 400 on empty bodies.

nodes.name: _hc_ping
nodes.summary: Pings healthchecks.io for the named script (start/success/fail), silently no-oping when unconfigured and swallowing network errors so a transient ping failure never breaks the underlying check.

nodes.name: state_get
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON-serialized state value for a key from the state directory, returning a default when the file is absent.

nodes.name: state_set
nodes.summary: Persists a JSON-serializable value under a key in the state directory, creating the directory if needed.

nodes.name: check_main
nodes.summary: Decorator factory wrapping a probe's main() to dedup failure alerts, fire recovery notifications, exit non-zero on error, and emit healthchecks.io start/success/fail pings as the dead-man side of monitoring.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Checks the upstream subscription auth flag via player_api and alerts on transition into or out of failed authentication.

nodes.name: _parse_canaries
nodes.summary: Stream-parses the XMLTV feed and accumulates per-canary-channel presence, future-programme counts, latest stop timestamp, and distinct programme titles.

nodes.name: _evaluate
nodes.summary: Evaluates a channel's accumulated EPG stats against future-programme, coverage-hours, and unique-title thresholds, returning health status plus a human-readable reason.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Fetches the XMLTV feed, evaluates each canary channel, prints a one-line status summary, and fires critical/recovery notifications on per-channel state transitions.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Fetches the local live-stream list, compares its length to the configured floor, and alerts on crossing below or back above the threshold.

nodes.name: latest_stop_ts
nodes.summary: Stream-parses the XMLTV feed and returns the maximum programme stop time as a Unix timestamp.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Computes how far the EPG extends into the future and warns when coverage falls below the configured minimum number of hours, recovering once when it returns.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads the subscription expiry, computes days remaining, and emits an at-most-once-daily renewal warning while within the warning window.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose project defining the cci-blackbook MCP service: builds the local image, wires Intel GPU device passthrough, persistent source/index/cache volumes, and Pangolin reverse-proxy routing labels with a /healthz health check.
nodes.languageNotes: Joins the external 'proxy' network and drives Pangolin via pangolin.proxy-resources.* labels rather than explicit port publishing (uses expose only).

nodes.name: cci-blackbook
nodes.summary: The cci-blackbook container service running the CCI Black Book MCP server on port 8000, with Intel renderD129 GPU passthrough, a read-only source PDF plus writable index/cache volumes, and Pangolin proxy labels exposing it at cci.ai.${DOMAIN}.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage build producing the cci-blackbook-mcp image: an Ubuntu 24.04 runtime with Intel OpenVINO GPU/OpenCL libraries and a uv-managed Python 3.12 virtualenv that installs the cci/app package and launches the cci-blackbook-mcp entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: Copies the uv binary from a dedicated stage; installs dependencies (pyproject.toml/uv.lock) before app source for layer-cache efficiency, then force-reinstalls onnxruntime-openvino pinned to the uv.lock version for Intel GPU inference.

nodes.name: uv
nodes.summary: Build stage pinned to the Astral uv 0.9.21 image, used solely to provide the uv and uvx binaries copied into the final runtime stage.

nodes.name: ubuntu
nodes.summary: Final runtime stage on Ubuntu 24.04 that installs Intel OpenVINO/OpenCL graphics libraries via the kobuk-team PPA, syncs the Python environment with uv, copies the cci_blackbook app, exposes port 8000, and runs the cci-blackbook-mcp command.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Custom Kanboard container image built on kanboard/kanboard:v1.2.51 that bakes in the OAuth2 and Model Context Protocol plugins (fetched from pinned GitHub releases/commits) and installs an OAuth configuration script plus a custom entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build using ADD with remote tarball URLs and build ARGs to pin plugin versions; plugins are duplicated into both /opt/kanboard/plugins and /var/www/app/plugins and ownership is handed to the nginx user.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage container image built on python:3.12-alpine that packages the kanban reference helper script and its projects.yaml config into /app and runs kanban_ref.py as the container entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal single-stage Alpine build; relies on the base image's bundled Python with no extra package installs.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Minimal single-stage Docker image based on nginx:1.27-alpine that bakes the kanban router's custom nginx configuration into the container, acting as the reverse proxy front for the Kanboard service.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build off the official alpine nginx image; the only build step is a COPY of default.conf into /etc/nginx/conf.d, so configuration is image-baked rather than mounted.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Alpine image (docker:27-cli) that adds supercronic and docker-cli-compose to build a cron-driven poller which redeploys the agent-kb service when its GHCR image digest moves. Isolates the read-write Docker socket here so the network-facing service-checks container never holds it.
nodes.languageNotes: Pins supercronic by version ARG and verifies the downloaded binary against a SHA1 ARG before chmod, a reproducible-build pattern. supercronic runs cron in the foreground in-process (no fork/setsid/setpgid) to stay compatible with Docker's default seccomp profile.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Alpine 3.20 container image for the events-watcher service, installing docker-cli, curl, and jq, then running watch.sh as its entrypoint to watch Docker events and fire Apprise notifications.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal single-stage build; bundles docker-cli so the entrypoint script can stream the host Docker event feed (expects the Docker socket mounted at runtime).

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Alpine-based cron container image that installs Python 3 plus HTTP/XML tooling (curl, jq, libxml2-utils) and a pinned supercronic binary, then runs the IPTV service-check probes on a schedule through the copied entrypoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses supercronic (version- and SHA1-pinned) instead of dcron because dcron's setpgid syscall is blocked by Docker's default seccomp profile; supercronic runs jobs in the foreground without fork/setsid/setpgid, avoiding a restart loop.

nodes.name: .yamllint.yml
nodes.summary: yamllint configuration extending the default ruleset, relaxing line-length and a few rules so the repo's docker-compose and template YAML pass the `make lint-yaml` gate.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Quick-reference contributor/agent guide describing every compose stack, the shared networks, the secrets workflow, the Makefile orchestration model, and Pangolin/Newt operational tips.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Master orchestrator that injects/syncs 1Password secrets and drives per-stack docker-compose up/down/restart across remote SSH Docker contexts, plus Keycloak realm migration, LDAP admin tooling, and lint/format targets.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-stack targets are generated dynamically in the included Makefile.include via STACK_RULES/SERVICE_RULES over the STACKS and SERVICES_<stack> lists; heavy use of shell define macros (READ_SECRET_FN) and $(shell) for context resolution.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview explaining the self-hosted streaming media platform: Pangolin edge proxy with Keycloak/OpenLDAP SSO, the per-project compose stacks, the request/auth flow, and how to expose new services via Pangolin Blueprints.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Repo-root ruff configuration (lint + format) applied to the project's Python sources under cci/, kanban/ref, and monitoring/service-checks via `make lint-py` and `make format`.

nodes.name: config.env
nodes.summary: Non-secret environment configuration for the core compose project, defining the shared compose project name, public domain, LDAP base DN, timezone, Keycloak admin user, MariaDB connection settings, and the Pangolin endpoint.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Core compose project defining the platform's foundational services: the Newt tunnel client that links to the Pangolin edge proxy, Keycloak authentication backed by MariaDB, an OpenLDAP directory, a Hugo homepage, the WUD update manager, and an MkDocs agent knowledge base.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Docker secrets mounted from ./secrets files and Pangolin proxy-resources labels to declaratively register each service's ingress domain, SSO policy, and health checks; persistent volumes are declared external.

nodes.name: newt
nodes.summary: Fosrl Newt tunnel client that establishes the connection to the Pangolin edge proxy, validating Docker networks and loading its NEWT_ID/NEWT_SECRET credentials from Docker secrets via a custom entrypoint wrapper.

nodes.name: auth
nodes.summary: Keycloak 26 identity provider serving SSO at auth.${DOMAIN}, configured for edge proxying behind Pangolin with the scripts feature enabled for a custom group-to-role mapper, and gated on the MariaDB db service being healthy.

nodes.name: db
nodes.summary: MariaDB 11.4 database backing Keycloak, with credentials supplied through Docker secret files and a SELECT 1 healthcheck that gates the dependent auth service.

nodes.name: ldap
nodes.summary: OpenLDAP directory server providing the dc=dephekt,dc=net base DN, with admin and config passwords loaded from Docker secrets and an ldapsearch-based healthcheck.

nodes.name: homepage
nodes.summary: Static Hugo homepage served at www.${DOMAIN} and the apex domain, using Pangolin rules to require Member-role SSO on /services while allowing public access elsewhere, plus apex-domain handling for .well-known Matrix delegation.

nodes.name: update-manager
nodes.summary: WUD (What's Up Docker) container that watches the Docker socket for image updates on a 12-hour interval, exposed at updates.stream.${DOMAIN} behind Pangolin SSO.

nodes.name: agent-kb
nodes.summary: MkDocs-based agent working-knowledge site served at agent-kb.${DOMAIN}, gated behind Pangolin SSO for the Member role.

nodes.name: newt-entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Entrypoint wrapper for the Newt container that exports NEWT_ID and NEWT_SECRET from Docker secret files into the environment before exec'ing the upstream Newt entrypoint.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose project defining the Immich self-hosted photo/video platform: the API/web server, machine-learning inference, PostgreSQL (pgvector/VectorChord), and Valkey (Redis), wired into the external Pangolin proxy network with Intel Arc hardware acceleration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Compose `extends` to merge in hardware-acceleration fragments and Pangolin/WUD label conventions for proxy routing and update tracking.

nodes.name: immich-server
nodes.summary: Immich API/web server container (port 2283) that extends the quicksync transcoding profile for Intel Arc QuickSync, mounts NFS-backed media volumes, and is exposed via Pangolin at photos.${DOMAIN}.

nodes.name: immich-machine-learning
nodes.summary: Immich machine-learning inference container using the OpenVINO image and the openvino hwaccel profile for Intel GPU-accelerated facial recognition, object detection, and smart search.

nodes.name: redis
nodes.summary: Valkey (Redis-compatible) container providing the job-queue backend for Immich, pinned to Valkey 9 by image digest.

nodes.name: database
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 14 container with pgvector/VectorChord extensions storing Immich metadata and AI search vectors, backed by a local SSD volume for performance.

nodes.name: hwaccel.ml.yml
nodes.summary: Hardware-acceleration override fragment defining machine-learning device profiles (armnn, rknn, cpu, cuda, rocm, openvino, openvino-wsl); the openvino profile is consumed by the immich-machine-learning service via Compose extends.

nodes.name: hwaccel.transcoding.yml
nodes.summary: Hardware-accelerated transcoding override fragment defining ffmpeg device profiles (cpu, nvenc, quicksync, rkmpp, vaapi, vaapi-wsl); the quicksync profile is consumed by the immich-server service for Intel Arc QuickSync.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Setup and operations guide for the Immich stack covering storage directories, environment configuration, deployment, Intel Arc QuickSync transcoding, OpenVINO ML acceleration, Pangolin integration, and Makefile commands.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose project 'matrix' defining a Tuwunel Matrix homeserver (built-in RocksDB) and an Element Web client, both exposed through Pangolin reverse-proxy labels on the external 'proxy' network.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Compose secrets (file-backed) for the OIDC client secret and Pangolin proxy-resource labels to declare external routing per service.

nodes.name: tuwunel
nodes.summary: Tuwunel Matrix homeserver container (Rust, built-in RocksDB) listening on port 8008, mounting tuwunel.toml read-only and a persistent data volume, with an OIDC client secret and Pangolin routing to matrix.${DOMAIN}.

nodes.name: element-web
nodes.summary: Element Web client container serving the Matrix UI, configured via a read-only element-web.json mount and exposed through Pangolin at chat.${DOMAIN}.

nodes.name: config.env
nodes.summary: Non-secret environment file for the Matrix stack, setting COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME and loaded as the env_file for the Tuwunel service.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Operator documentation for the Matrix stack covering services, required secrets, deploy commands, Tuwunel configuration, first-time setup, Keycloak OIDC/SSO, federation, and Element Web.

nodes.name: config.env
nodes.summary: Environment variable file defining the base DOMAIN (dephekt.net) that is interpolated into the media compose stack's Pangolin proxy labels and per-service public domains.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose project for the self-hosted media stack, defining the Jellyfin streaming server, Radarr/Sonarr media managers, NZBGet Usenet downloader, and Jellyseerr request portal, all wired into the external Pangolin proxy and shared core networks with per-service healthchecks.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pangolin reverse-proxy labels and WUD (What's Up Docker) tag-include/transform regex labels to manage update detection per service.

nodes.name: jellyfin
nodes.summary: Jellyfin media server (10.11.8) with GPU-accelerated transcoding via /dev/dri, exposed on ports 8096 and 7359/udp, proxied at stream.${DOMAIN} with a /health container healthcheck.

nodes.name: radarr
nodes.summary: Radarr movie collection manager (LinuxServer image) proxied at movies.stream.${DOMAIN} with SSO auto-login and a /ping healthcheck on port 7878.

nodes.name: sonarr
nodes.summary: Sonarr TV series collection manager (LinuxServer image) proxied at shows.stream.${DOMAIN} with SSO auto-login and a /ping healthcheck on port 8989.

nodes.name: nzbget
nodes.summary: NZBGet Usenet download client (LinuxServer image) proxied at grabs.stream.${DOMAIN} with SSO auto-login and a root-path healthcheck on port 6789, mounting the shared downloads volume.

nodes.name: seerr
nodes.summary: Jellyseerr media request portal (pinned by image digest) proxied at requests.stream.${DOMAIN} with Member-role SSO and a /api/v1/status healthcheck on port 5055.

nodes.name: jellyfin-web-config.json
nodes.summary: Jellyfin web client configuration defining the selectable color themes, a Requests menu link to the Jellyseerr portal, and the enabled client-side player and screensaver plugins.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose project for the homelab monitoring stack, defining the apprise-api notification router plus three custom probe/automation containers (events-watcher, service-checks, agent-kb-redeploy) wired onto the monitoring and Pangolin proxy networks with file-backed secrets and a shared state volume.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Docker Compose file-backed secrets, an external `proxy` network shared with Pangolin, Pangolin proxy-resource labels for ingress/SSO, and WUD tag-filter labels for image update detection.

nodes.name: apprise-api
nodes.summary: Central notification router (caronc/apprise) that accepts tagged HTTP POSTs on :8000 and fans them out to Discord, Matrix, and ntfy.sh; exposed publicly via Pangolin SSO (Member role) and health-checked on its /status endpoint.

nodes.name: events-watcher
nodes.summary: Custom container that tails Docker health_status events and POSTs critical/info notifications to apprise-api, deduping via the shared monitoring-state volume and pinging Healthchecks.io as a dead-man heartbeat for the daemon itself.

nodes.name: service-checks
nodes.summary: Supercronic-driven cron host running the Python IPTV probe scripts, configured with upstream/local Xtream-Codes URLs, EPG/channel thresholds, and per-check Healthchecks.io ping URLs, notifying failures through apprise-api.

nodes.name: agent-kb-redeploy
nodes.summary: Scheduled container that pulls the latest agent-kb image from GHCR and recreates it via the read-only core compose project, posting a redeploy notification to apprise-api; the only container granted a read-write Docker socket.

nodes.name: config.env
nodes.summary: Shared environment file for the monitoring stack, supplying the TZ timezone variable consumed by every compose service via env_file.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Architecture documentation for the monitoring stack, describing the Apprise tag-routing fan-out to Discord/Matrix/ntfy, the local probe containers, the UptimeRobot and Healthchecks.io SaaS witnesses, and the 'constellation-of-alerts' diagnostic truth table.

nodes.name: .shellcheckrc
nodes.summary: ShellCheck configuration that sets the POSIX sh dialect and disables the unused-variable warning (SC2034) repo-wide for the stack's shell scripts.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker and barrel for the CCI Black Book MCP service, exposing the cci_blackbook module namespace.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project metadata for the CCI Black Book MCP service, declaring dependencies, the console-script entry point, dependency groups, and the Hatchling wheel build backend.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the CCI Black Book MCP service covering its private-data handling, deployment, MCP client setup, and the search/read citation tools it exposes.

nodes.name: test_blackbook.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the CCI Black Book service verifying page chunking, bearer-token auth, bounded cited search results, citation reads, and graceful abstention when the indexed source PDF is missing.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard library unittest with tempfile-backed fixtures; exercises the cci_blackbook modules end to end.

nodes.name: BlackBookServiceTest
nodes.summary: Test case that indexes a temporary PDF-backed source and asserts the Black Book service returns bounded cited search evidence, bounded citation reads, and abstains when the index source is missing.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project running the Channels DVR media server in host-network mode with Intel GPU passthrough for hardware transcoding and bind-mounted config/recordings storage.

nodes.name: channels-dvr
nodes.summary: Channels DVR container using host networking and /dev/dri GPU passthrough, persisting config and recordings to bind-mounted NAS paths.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project for the grow-app HMI site, wiring it to the shared MQTT broker network and injecting MQTT and GHCR-OCI firmware-distribution secrets via Docker secrets.

nodes.name: grow-app-site
nodes.summary: LAN-local grow-app site HMI container connecting to the mosquitto-site MQTT broker and pulling firmware packages from GHCR as OCI artifacts, with a Node fetch-based health check.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the grow stack describing the grow-app site HMI service, its required MQTT/firmware secrets, and how to deploy it.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project running IPTVBoss behind Pangolin/Keycloak SSO, exposing both the noVNC management UI and the XtremeCodes guide/playlist server through Pangolin proxy-resource labels.

nodes.name: iptvboss
nodes.summary: IPTVBoss container serving its noVNC UI (proxied via Pangolin SSO) and an XtremeCodes server for playlist/guide data, with a cron-driven 3-hourly data refresh and NFS-mounted state.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project for the Kanboard kanban stack: an nginx router, the Kanboard app with OAuth2, one-shot admin and OAuth init jobs, and the kanban-ref redirect service, fronted by Pangolin.

nodes.name: kanban-router
nodes.summary: nginx reverse-proxy container that fronts the Kanboard app and the kanban-ref service, exposed to Pangolin and gated on the kanboard health check.

nodes.name: kanboard
nodes.summary: Kanboard application container built from the local kanboard-oauth2 image with trusted-proxy header handling and a persistent data volume.

nodes.name: kanboard-admin-init
nodes.summary: One-shot init container that resets the Kanboard admin password from a secret via the Kanboard CLI once the app is healthy.

nodes.name: kanboard-oauth-init
nodes.summary: One-shot init container that runs configure-oauth.php to provision Kanboard's Keycloak OAuth2 client settings once the app is healthy.

nodes.name: kanban-ref
nodes.summary: Python redirect microservice that resolves Forgejo/Kanboard task references into task URLs by querying the Kanboard JSON-RPC API.

nodes.name: configure-oauth.php
nodes.summary: Kanboard bootstrap script that writes OAuth2/OIDC client settings into the Kanboard database from environment variables and prunes legacy access-control groups.

nodes.name: cleanup_legacy_groups
nodes.summary: Removes legacy Kanboard groups identified by external IDs, deleting their membership rows and group records via prepared statements.

nodes.name: entrypoint-with-plugins.sh
nodes.summary: Kanboard image entrypoint wrapper that syncs bundled plugins into the app plugins directory with correct ownership before delegating to the upstream entrypoint.

nodes.name: configure-kanboard-client.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent Keycloak admin-API bootstrap that provisions the Kanboard OIDC client, client roles, member groups, and a custom authentication/gate flow, then stores the resulting client secret.
nodes.languageNotes: POSIX sh driving the Keycloak admin REST API via curl/jq with many small helper functions composing into ensure_* idempotent provisioning steps.

nodes.name: kc
nodes.summary: Curl wrapper that issues authenticated requests against the Keycloak admin REST API, handling the bearer token and base URL.

nodes.name: ensure_client
nodes.summary: Creates or updates the Kanboard OIDC client in Keycloak with the desired redirect URIs and settings, returning its UUID.

nodes.name: disable_client_pkce
nodes.summary: Clears the PKCE challenge-method attribute on the Kanboard client so the OAuth2 flow matches Kanboard's expectations.

nodes.name: ensure_group
nodes.summary: Creates a Keycloak realm group by name if it does not already exist and returns its id.

nodes.name: copy_group_members
nodes.summary: Copies all members from one Keycloak group into another, used when migrating users between access groups.

nodes.name: remove_group_mapper
nodes.summary: Deletes a legacy group-membership protocol mapper from the Kanboard client when tearing down the old access model.

nodes.name: ensure_auth_flow
nodes.summary: Creates or locates the top-level Keycloak authentication flow used for Kanboard access gating.

nodes.name: ensure_gate_flow
nodes.summary: Builds the sub-flow that gates Kanboard logins on a required client role, wiring its executions into the parent auth flow.

nodes.name: ensure_flow_execution
nodes.summary: Idempotently adds and configures an execution within a Keycloak authentication flow, setting its provider and requirement.

nodes.name: store_client_secret
nodes.summary: Fetches the generated client secret from Keycloak and writes it to the Kanboard secrets file for the OAuth2 init job.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the kanban stack covering its router/Kanboard/ref services, the Keycloak SSO authentication model, required secrets, and deployment steps.

nodes.name: kanban_ref.py
nodes.summary: Stdlib HTTP redirect microservice that maps Forgejo/Kanboard task references to task URLs, querying the Kanboard JSON-RPC API and choosing public vs authenticated URLs per project.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure standard-library implementation: http.server BaseHTTPRequestHandler plus urllib for both the JSON-RPC client and request handling, no third-party web framework.

nodes.name: load_projects
nodes.summary: Loads the projects mapping from the YAML config file, falling back to a minimal hand-rolled parser when PyYAML is unavailable.

nodes.name: parse_minimal_projects
nodes.summary: Minimal dependency-free parser for the projects YAML, extracting the project-prefix-to-record mapping line by line.

nodes.name: api_call
nodes.summary: Issues a JSON-RPC 2.0 request to the Kanboard API using urllib with basic-auth from the API token, raising KanboardError on transport or RPC errors.

nodes.name: task_url
nodes.summary: Selects between the public and authenticated Kanboard task URL builders based on whether the project record marks the project public.

nodes.name: Handler
nodes.summary: BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass that parses incoming reference paths, resolves them to Kanboard tasks via the API, and issues redirects or JSON responses including a healthz endpoint.

nodes.name: projects.yaml
nodes.summary: Project reference mapping consumed by the kanban-ref service, associating Forgejo project prefixes with Kanboard project records.

nodes.name: test_kanban_ref.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the kanban-ref service that spin up the HTTP handler against a mocked Kanboard API and assert correct redirects, reference fallbacks, and error handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ThreadingHTTPServer in a background thread plus unittest.mock to stub the Kanboard JSON-RPC client and environment.

nodes.name: KanbanRefHandlerTest
nodes.summary: Test case that runs the kanban-ref HTTP handler against a mocked Kanboard API, verifying public/private redirect behavior, reference padding fallbacks, and unknown-prefix handling.

nodes.name: default.conf
nodes.summary: nginx server config for the kanban-router that proxies /i/ to the kanban-ref redirect service and everything else to the Kanboard app, forwarding host and protocol headers.

nodes.name: Makefile.include
nodes.summary: Reusable Make macros that generate per-stack and per-service up/down/pull/restart/logs targets from the STACKS and SERVICES_* lists, wiring docker compose context and secrets dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of GNU Make define/call/eval/foreach metaprogramming to synthesize targets across all compose stacks.

nodes.name: element-web.json
nodes.summary: Element Web client configuration pinning the default homeserver and forcing SSO-only login by disabling password and third-party-id login.

nodes.name: tuwunel.toml
nodes.summary: Tuwunel (Matrix homeserver) configuration defining the server name, well-known delegation, and the Keycloak OIDC identity provider for federated SSO login.

nodes.name: crontab
nodes.summary: Supercronic crontab that runs redeploy.sh every three minutes to poll GHCR and re-pull the agent-kb image when it changes.

nodes.name: entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Entrypoint that logs into GHCR with a read token so compose pulls can fetch the private agent-kb image, then hands off to supercronic with the bundled crontab.

nodes.name: redeploy.sh
nodes.summary: Scoped redeploy script that pulls the agent-kb image and recreates the container only when the resolved image changed, notifying apprise on an actual image swap.

nodes.name: load-config.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent helper that POSTs monitoring.yaml to the apprise-api container under the 'monitoring' token so notification routing persists across restarts.

nodes.name: monitoring.yaml.template
nodes.summary: Apprise notification routing template rendered to monitoring.yaml at deploy time. Maps alert tags (iptv, cam, critical/warning/info, heartbeat) to ntfy.sh, Discord, Matrix, and a hc.io dead-man heartbeat, with {{...}} placeholders filled from 1Password.
nodes.languageNotes: Placeholders use {{VAR}} braces substituted by render-config.sh because Apprise YAML does not support runtime ${VAR} interpolation.

nodes.name: render-config.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX sh script that reads ntfy topics, Discord webhooks, Matrix tokens, and Healthchecks.io ping URLs from 1Password and renders both monitoring.yaml (apprise routing) and secrets/healthchecks.env. Invoked by `make inject-secrets`.

nodes.name: parse_discord_webhook
nodes.summary: Reads a full Discord webhook URL from a 1Password reference and splits it into the id/token components Apprise's discord:// scheme expects, exiting on a parse failure.

nodes.name: watch.sh
nodes.summary: Long-running daemon that watches the Docker health_status event stream and POSTs critical/recovery notifications to apprise-api, persisting per-container last-known status to dedupe alerts. Also runs a background hc.io heartbeat. Deployed by the events-watcher Dockerfile.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a detached subshell heartbeat and a reconnect loop so a hung curl or a dockerd restart cannot stall or kill the event stream.

nodes.name: emit
nodes.summary: Posts a tagged JSON notification to apprise-api, substituting a placeholder body because apprise-api rejects empty bodies with HTTP 400, and warns instead of failing if the POST errors.

nodes.name: crontab
nodes.summary: Supercronic crontab scheduling the hourly IPTV health checks (auth, channel-count, EPG freshness, canary EPG), a daily renewal warning, and a 5-minute apprise-pipeline dead-man heartbeat POST.

nodes.name: entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Minimal container entrypoint that execs supercronic on the bind-mounted crontab, running scheduled checks in-process to avoid the fork/setsid that Docker's default seccomp profile blocks.

nodes.name: acl
nodes.summary: Mosquitto ACL for the central aggregator broker, scoping the per-site bridge user (bridge-daniel-home) to its own grow/daniel-home/# subtree as defense-in-depth against cross-site leakage.

nodes.name: mosquitto.conf
nodes.summary: Mosquitto config for the central aggregator broker: anonymous access disabled, password and ACL files under /run/mosquitto, and persistence enabled. Receives opted-in sites' subtrees via per-site bridges.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project defining two Mosquitto 2.x brokers (site and central) on a shared grow-mqtt network with a durable site-to-central bridge, file-based secrets, and per-broker healthchecks.

nodes.name: mosquitto-site
nodes.summary: Site broker: Daniel's local grow/daniel-home/# bus, exposed on host port 1883 for LAN ESPHome devices and bridging up to the central aggregator. Runs via site-entrypoint.sh.

nodes.name: mosquitto-central
nodes.summary: Central aggregator broker with no host port, reachable only over the grow-mqtt docker network and receiving each site's subtree via its per-site bridge credential. Runs via central-entrypoint.sh.

nodes.name: central-entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Entrypoint for the central broker: hashes the bridge user password into /run/mosquitto/passwd, copies the ACL, fixes ownership for the mosquitto uid, and execs mosquitto with the central config.

nodes.name: site-entrypoint.sh
nodes.summary: Entrypoint for the site broker: hashes the edge and grow-app passwords, renders the runtime config from the template by appending the bridge remote_password from the secret, fixes ownership, and execs mosquitto.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the two-broker Mosquitto stack: the site and central brokers, the user/credential matrix, the 1Password-injected secrets, and the make targets to deploy and connect LAN devices.

nodes.name: acl
nodes.summary: Mosquitto ACL for the site broker scoping the edge ESPHome devices (edge-daniel-home) and the local grow-app server (grow-app-site-daniel-home) to the grow/daniel-home/# subtree.

nodes.name: mosquitto.conf.tmpl
nodes.summary: Site broker config template with the durable bridge connection up to mosquitto-central; the bridge remote_password is appended at runtime by site-entrypoint.sh from the bridge secret.

nodes.name: config.yml.template
nodes.summary: Pangolin application config template defining gerbil/app/domain/server/email/flags settings for the dephekt.net edge, with {{SERVER_SECRET}}/{{SMTP_USER}}/{{SMTP_PASS}} placeholders rendered from 1Password.

nodes.name: render-config.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX sh script that reads the Pangolin server secret and SMTP credentials from 1Password and substitutes them into config.yml.template to produce the gitignored config.yml. Invoked by `make inject-secrets`.

nodes.name: dynamic_config.yml
nodes.summary: Traefik file-provider dynamic config defining the badger forward-auth middleware, HTTPS redirect, and the routers/services that proxy the Pangolin dashboard, API, and WebSocket traffic to pangolin:3002/3000.

nodes.name: traefik_config.yml
nodes.summary: Traefik static config: HTTP+file providers, the badger plugin, the Let's Encrypt cloudflare DNS-01 resolver, web/websecure entrypoints with HTTP/3, and the ping healthcheck for the Pangolin edge.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project for the Pangolin edge stack (pangolin control plane, gerbil WireGuard tunnel, traefik proxy) deployed over the pangolin-edge SSH context to the VPS, using absolute /opt/pangolin bind mounts.
nodes.languageNotes: Bind mounts use absolute /opt/pangolin paths because the remote Docker context resolves relative paths against the local filesystem, which would mount empty directories on the edge.

nodes.name: pangolin
nodes.summary: Pangolin EE control-plane container serving the dashboard, API, and traefik-config provider on port 3003 with a memory-limited deploy block and an API healthcheck.

nodes.name: gerbil
nodes.summary: Gerbil WireGuard gateway container that holds the edge's public ports (443, 80, 51820/udp) and pulls its remote config from Pangolin; traefik shares its network namespace.

nodes.name: traefik
nodes.summary: Traefik v3.6 reverse proxy sharing gerbil's network namespace, loading traefik_config.yml and the cloudflare DNS token env_file, and terminating TLS for the Pangolin edge.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the Pangolin edge deployment: the pangolin-edge SSH Docker context workflow, the untracked /opt/pangolin runtime state, the inject-secrets render flow, and EE license handling.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose project for the self-hosted Penpot design workspace: frontend, backend, exporter, MCP, PostgreSQL, and Valkey, wired for Keycloak OIDC auth and exposed via Pangolin proxy-resource labels.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YAML anchors (x-penpot-flags / x-penpot-public-uri / x-penpot-body-size) merged into multiple services to share environment configuration.

nodes.name: penpot-frontend
nodes.summary: Penpot frontend container serving the web UI on port 9001, fronted by Pangolin proxy-resource labels for design.ai.${DOMAIN}, and depending on the backend, exporter, and MCP services.

nodes.name: penpot-backend
nodes.summary: Penpot backend application wired to PostgreSQL and Valkey, with filesystem asset storage and full Keycloak OIDC settings for the home realm; depends on healthy postgres and valkey.

nodes.name: penpot-mcp
nodes.summary: Penpot built-in MCP server container exposing Model Context Protocol access to the design workspace on the internal penpot network.

nodes.name: penpot-exporter
nodes.summary: Penpot exporter container that renders design exports, pointed at the frontend public URI and Valkey, and depending on a healthy valkey.

nodes.name: penpot-postgres
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 15 database for Penpot with data-checksums, a pg_isready healthcheck, and a persistent named volume holding the penpot database.

nodes.name: penpot-valkey
nodes.summary: Valkey 8.1 cache/queue backend for Penpot with a memory cap and volatile-lfu eviction, exposing a PONG healthcheck on the internal penpot network.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the Penpot stack: public/internal URLs, Keycloak OIDC integration, 1Password-rendered secrets, deploy make targets, and backup/restore notes for the postgres and assets volumes.

layers.name: Edge & Ingress
layers.description: The internet-facing reverse-proxy and tunnel control plane: the Pangolin/Gerbil/Traefik edge stack that terminates TLS and routes traffic, plus the Newt tunnel client that links the core stack to the edge.

layers.name: Identity & Access
layers.description: The shared SSO foundation in the core stack: Keycloak, its MariaDB store, and OpenLDAP, plus the Keycloak client-provisioning bootstrap that wires Kanboard into single sign-on.

layers.name: Media & Streaming
layers.description: Photo, video, and live-TV delivery stacks: Jellyfin/Jellyseerr/*arr/NZBGet, Immich photo management, IPTVBoss, and Channels DVR with their hardware-acceleration overrides.

layers.name: Communication & Messaging
layers.description: Real-time human and machine messaging: the Matrix/Element chat homeserver, the two-broker Mosquitto MQTT grow-control spine, and the LAN-local grow-app HMI that rides it.

layers.name: Collaboration & Productivity
layers.description: Self-hosted team productivity apps fronted by Keycloak SSO: the Kanboard kanban stack (router, app, ref redirect, OAuth bootstrap) and the Penpot design workspace.

layers.name: Knowledge & Content Services
layers.description: Content- and knowledge-serving applications: the CCI Black Book FastMCP retrieval service, the MkDocs agent-knowledge site, and the Hugo public homepage.

layers.name: Monitoring & Operations
layers.description: Observability and self-healing automation: the Apprise notification router, Docker health-event watcher, IPTV canary probes, agent-kb redeploy job, and the WUD image-update manager.

layers.name: Orchestration & Build
layers.description: Repo-root tooling that orchestrates and lints every independent compose project: the master Makefile, reusable Make macros, and the Python/YAML/shell linter configs.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Per-stack operator READMEs, the root project overview, and the AGENTS.md contributor/agent reference guide.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the root README, the project's own story. media-stack is a self-hosted streaming media platform whose goal is a polished, no-client-software experience that hides operational complexity from family and friends. The README lays out the two ideas everything else builds on: the Pangolin edge reverse-proxy authenticates every request and routes it to each stack's Newt tunnel client, and Keycloak (backed by OpenLDAP) is the single identity provider. Read this first to get the map before the territory.

tour.title: The Master Orchestrator
tour.description: Each top-level directory is an independent Docker Compose project; the root Makefile is what stitches them into one platform. It injects and syncs 1Password secrets, drives docker-compose up/down/restart across remote SSH Docker contexts, and runs Keycloak realm migration and LDAP tooling. The reusable macros in Makefile.include generate per-stack and per-service targets from STACKS and SERVICES_* lists, so a new stack inherits up/down/pull/logs targets for free. This is the entry point an operator actually types against.
tour.languageLesson: Make macros (defined with `define`/`$(eval ...)`) let one template expand into many targets from a list variable, and `.PHONY` targets name actions (like `up`/`down`) that aren't files. This is how a single Makefile parameterizes identical orchestration logic across every compose project without duplication.

tour.title: Edge & Ingress: How Requests Enter
tour.description: Building on the README's request flow, this is the front door. The Pangolin compose project deploys over a dedicated SSH context to the VPS and runs three cooperating pieces: the Pangolin control plane, the Gerbil WireGuard tunnel, and Traefik. Traefik's static config terminates TLS with a Let's Encrypt Cloudflare DNS-01 resolver and defines the web/websecure entrypoints, while its dynamic config wires the Badger forward-auth middleware that gates traffic. Back inside the core stack, the Newt tunnel client dials out to Pangolin and is how every home service becomes reachable without opening inbound ports.
tour.languageLesson: Traefik splits configuration into static (entrypoints, providers, TLS resolvers — loaded once at boot) and dynamic (routers, services, middlewares — hot-reloaded from the file provider). Forward-auth middleware lets the proxy delegate the authn decision to an external service before any request reaches the backend.

tour.title: Identity & Access: Keycloak + LDAP
tour.description: With requests arriving, who is allowed in? The core stack hosts the shared SSO foundation: Keycloak 26 serving auth.${DOMAIN}, its MariaDB store, and an OpenLDAP directory. Keycloak is the OIDC/SAML IdP that OIDC-native apps like Immich and Pangolin talk to directly, while it uses OpenLDAP as its password backend so LDAP-only apps like Jellyfin can federate too. The non-secret config.env file declares the shared domain, LDAP base DN, and the Keycloak/MariaDB wiring that the auth service depends on. Group membership defined here (streaming, admin) is what later steps' services check for access.
tour.languageLesson: Compose `depends_on` with a `condition: service_healthy` clause sequences startup so Keycloak only boots after MariaDB's healthcheck passes. Without health-gated dependencies, the auth service would race its own database and crash-loop on first boot.

tour.title: Media & Streaming
tour.description: Now the reason the platform exists. The media compose project runs Jellyfin for streaming alongside the *arr stack (Radarr/Sonarr) and the Jellyseerr request portal, all on the shared proxy and core networks. Jellyfin transcodes with GPU acceleration via /dev/dri and is exposed at stream.${DOMAIN}; its web client config adds theming and a Requests menu link into Jellyseerr. The separate Immich project provides photo management, with its server extending an Intel QuickSync transcoding profile and surfacing at photos.${DOMAIN}. These are the user-facing apps the SSO and edge layers exist to protect.

tour.title: Exposing a Service the Blueprints Way
tour.description: How does a new container become a public, SSO-protected URL? The README calls this pattern Blueprints: you don't edit the proxy, you add Docker labels and Newt autodiscovers them. Sonarr declares shows.stream.${DOMAIN} with SSO auto-login, Jellyseerr declares requests.stream.${DOMAIN} gated to the Member role, and the CCI Black Book service declares cci.ai.${DOMAIN} with its own access policy. Each is the same recipe from Steps 3 and 4 applied: labels name the hostname and the Keycloak role, and the tunnel client wires it through Pangolin automatically.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose `labels` are arbitrary key/value metadata attached to a container; a sidecar like Newt reads the label set at runtime to build proxy routes (hostname, target port, required SSO role). This label-as-configuration convention is how new services self-register with the edge instead of requiring central proxy edits.

tour.title: The CCI Black Book MCP Service
tour.description: Most stacks are off-the-shelf images, but CCI Black Book is a real application that lives in this repo, so it's the best place to read actual code. mcp_server.py is the FastMCP/Starlette entry point exposing ask/search/read_citation/status as MCP tools behind bearer-auth middleware; it delegates to service.py, the orchestration layer that ingests a source PDF and serves hybrid retrieval with reciprocal-rank fusion; which in turn sits on store.py, a SQLite index combining FTS5 full-text search with brute-force vector cosine search. The multi-stage Dockerfile and the CCI README show how this Python package is packaged and operated.
tour.languageLesson: The Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build: an Ubuntu base layer pulls in Intel OpenVINO GPU/OpenCL libraries, then a uv-managed Python 3.12 virtualenv installs the app package, so build-time tooling stays out of the runtime image. uv resolves and installs dependencies far faster than pip and produces a reproducible lockfile-driven environment.

tour.title: Monitoring & Notifications
tour.description: A platform serving family needs to self-report problems. The monitoring stack centers on apprise-api, a notification router that accepts tagged HTTP POSTs and fans them out to Discord, Matrix, and ntfy.sh. Around it sit custom probes: the IPTV canaries (such as the EPG canary that stream-parses a 12 MB XMLTV feed for six channels) and the events-watcher that tails Docker health_status events. They all share _lib.py, which provides the notify/state helpers and a check_main decorator that wraps each probe with deduplicated failure-and-recovery alerting, plus Healthchecks.io dead-man pings so a dead watcher is itself noticed.
tour.languageLesson: A Python decorator (`@check_main`) wraps each probe's entry function with shared cross-cutting behavior — running the check, persisting state to disk, deduplicating repeat alerts, and pinging a dead-man monitor — so each individual canary file stays a few lines of probe-specific logic. This is the decorator pattern used to factor out boilerplate.

tour.title: Communication & Messaging
tour.description: Beyond media, the platform hosts real-time messaging for both humans and machines. The matrix stack runs a Tuwunel homeserver with an Element Web client, both exposed through Pangolin labels like every other service. The mqtt stack is a different shape: two Mosquitto brokers (site and central) joined by a durable bridge, forming the messaging spine for grow automation. The grow-app-site HMI rides that spine, subscribing to the site broker and pulling firmware from GHCR as OCI artifacts. Together they show the same edge/SSO conventions extending to chat and IoT.

tour.title: Collaboration & Productivity
tour.description: The collaboration stacks close the loop back to Keycloak from Step 4. The Kanboard kanban project bundles an nginx router, the app with OAuth2, and one-shot init jobs; its access is provisioned by configure-kanboard-client.sh, an idempotent Keycloak admin-API bootstrap that creates the OIDC client, client roles, member groups, and a custom gate flow. The Penpot design workspace follows the same model — frontend, backend, exporter, MCP, Postgres, and Valkey, all wired for Keycloak OIDC and exposed via Pangolin labels. This is the SSO-everywhere principle applied to productivity tools.

tour.title: Wrap-Up: The Operator's Map
tour.description: AGENTS.md is the capstone: a single quick-reference describing every compose stack, the shared networks, the 1Password secrets workflow, and the Makefile orchestration model from Step 2, with Pangolin/Newt operational tips. Having walked the edge, identity, media, application code, monitoring, messaging, and collaboration layers, this document ties them together and is where a new contributor or agent confirms the conventions before making a change. From here you understand not just what each stack does, but the repeating pattern — label-driven exposure, Keycloak SSO, Make-driven orchestration — that makes the whole platform coherent.
